CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-31-2018 Continued To Feb-27-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prove-Up Hearing On Application For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant ARTtwo50, Inc., a Delaware corporation.
Dept. 514. Judge: A. James Robertson, II. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Joshua K. Haevernick, Esq. appeared for plaintiff IKH Holdings Ltd., a BVI limited company.
No appearance by defaulted defendant ARTtwo50, Inc., a Delaware corporation.
Plaintiff's complaint for Breach of Written Contract was filed on May 11, 2018, and personally served on defendant ARTtwo50, Inc., a Delaware corporation, on May 23, 2018.
Default as to defendant ARTtwo50, Inc., a Delaware corporation was entered on June 28, 2018.
DOE defendants were dismissed on September 27, 2018.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 9, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff IKH Holdings Ltd., a BVI limited company, and against the defaulted defendant ARTtwo50, Inc., a Delaware corporation, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $500,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $104,216.92.
Attorney Fees - $41,870.00.
Costs - $3,667.76.

Total - $649,754.68.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF IKH HOLDINGS LTD., A BVI LIMITED COMPANY's Motion To Seal Note Purchase Agreement, Convertible Promissory Note, Amendment To Convertible Promissory Note.

IKH's motion to seal is GRANTED. No opposition filed and the motion complies with CRC 2.550 et seq.

Counsel for IKH is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, identifies each document or portion of a document that is to be sealed, and complies with CRC 2.550(d).
